content.value: Monsters are an essential part of Heroic Adventures, providing challenges, adversaries, and opportunities for adventurers. Monsters come in a wide variety of forms, from legendary creatures to common foes, and serve different purposes in the game world.

: Monsters can serve as adversaries for the player characters, providing challenges and obstacles to overcome. They can be powerful creatures, such as dragons or giants, or smaller foes, such as goblins or skeletons. These encounters can test the players combat skills, strategies, and resource management, adding excitement and tension to the gameplay.

: Some monsters are not necessarily adversaries in the traditional sense, but rather environmental hazards or obstacles. For example, a treacherous swamp might be home to giant crocodiles or poisonous snakes, or a dense forest might be inhabited by hostile creatures like trolls or sproutlings. These monsters can add depth and complexity to the game world, forcing the players to navigate dangerous terrain and make strategic decisions to avoid or defeat them.

: Monsters can offer rich role-playing opportunities for players, allowing them to interact with unique and memorable characters. For example, a mind slayer or an ocean lord might provide opportunities for diplomacy, negotiation, or creative problem-solving, challenging the players to think beyond combat and use their wits or social skills to overcome obstacles.

: Monsters often guard valuable treasure, magical items, or rare resources that the players can obtain by defeating them. This can provide a strong incentive for adventurers to seek out and defeat powerful monsters and adds an element of risk versus reward to the gameplay. Monsters can also drop valuable components, such as ether and scroll fragments which can be used for crafting.

: Most monsters are roughly the size of a human and take up 1 space on the game board. Other monsters are much bigger, such as ogres, giants, and dragons, and can take up 2 spaces, a 2x2 grid of spaces, or more. It is up to the game master to decide how much space a monster takes up on the board, which is largely dependent on what miniatures you have available.

: One of the joys of tabletop roleplaying is the ability to create your own monsters, either by modifying existing ones or inventing entirely new creatures. This allows the game master and players to exercise their creativity and tailor the game world to their own liking. Homebrew monsters can be used to create unique challenges, puzzles, or encounters that reflect the gamemasters or players imagination and storytelling style.

: Some monsters have access to mana and spells and will be listed in the monster’s feature list. In this case, monsters can cast spells following the normal rules and can apply one enhancement per monster level.
: When needing to know a monster’s strength, refer to the static bonus to in their damage stat, ignoring and indicated dice. So, if a monster’s damage is 2d6+5, then the monster’s strength is 5.

Monster profiles can be used to quickly determine the stats for a wide range of unique monsters that fit your adventure. Each level range has statistics for each of the monster profiles listed here. This means for low levels, you might use the soldier profile for goblins, the leader profile for the goblin chief, the brute profile for orcs, and the behemoth profile for ogres. At higher levels you might use the minion profile for the same orcs, use the brute profile for ogres, and the leader profile for the ogre chieftain.

Minions are weak and fragile creatures that follow the orders of others. They are often used to soak up the damage of opponents and often their only strategy is to use the sheer force of numbers to defeat their enemies.
Examples of minions for low level characters is untrained goblins, spiders, and rats. At higher levels minions could represent orc soldiers or demonic hordes.
Soldiers are creatures trained for war. They fight as a unit, use advanced tactics, and come to the fight prepared with weapons and armor.
Examples of soldiers are trained goblins and orc warriors and town guards.

Beasts are creatures that are tough and dangerous, but have low intelligence, do not communicate with other creatures or use tactics, and are generally set lose against enemy combatants but not easily controlled.
Examples of beasts are wolves, giant spiders, alligators, and lions.
Brutes are tough and do a lot of damage. They are usually few in number and coordinate with soldiers under the direction of leaders to act as the hammer blow against their enemies.
Examples of brutes at low levels could be orcs and demons. At high levels brutes could be ogres and trolls.
Behemoths are large creatures that are very tough and do devastating damage. Often there is only or two behemoths in a combat.
At low levels behemoths are things like ogres and trolls. At high levels a behemoth could be a gargantuan spider, drake, or giants.
Slayers are highly skilled and do high damage, but in general are less armored than brutes and soldiers. They are used to stand toe to toe with the best of the enemy combatants.
Examples of slayers are assassins, skilled warriors and ruthless barbarians.
Commanders have high stats, provide direction to their allies, and control enemy combatants in various ways in order to manipulate the combat to their advantage.
Examples of leaders are chieftains and captains.
Leaders have average stats and are generally vulnerable in combat but often have access to spells and abilities to damage the enemy and help their allies.
Examples of mages are orc shamans and human wizards.

Monsters will have various features similar to player characters. These features mean that not all level 4 brutes are the same, for example. Each monster feature will have a recommend level range. This level range is only a recommendation, and more powerful abilities can be given to monsters outside of the recommended range. If a monster has a heroic action but not the “Heroic (x)” feature, then it is assumed that they have access to a single use of that heroic action.
This creature’s skill is x instead of what is listed on the combat profile
This creature’s agility is x instead of what is listed on the combat profile
This creature’s aim is x instead of what is listed on the combat profile
This creature’s tactics is x instead of what is listed on the combat profile
This creature’s intelligence is x instead of what is listed on the combat profile
This creature’s willpower is x instead of what is listed on the combat profile
This creature’s strength is x instead of what is listed on the combat profile
This creature’s initiative is x instead of what is listed on the combat profile
This creature’s toughness is x instead of what is listed on the combat profile
This creature’s block is x instead of what is listed on the combat profile
This creature’s damage is x instead of what is listed on the combat profile
Gain a ranged attack with x damage and a range of 20 spaces.
Make a ranged attack that does damage equal to your standard melee attack and a range of 6 spaces.
This creature’s health is x instead of what is listed on the combat profile
This creature’s major movement is x spaces, and its minor movement is y spaces
Gain x uses of heroic actions
Make an attack with an extra damage dice
Make an attack with two extra damage dice
Choose a space within 10 spaces. Make an intelligence vs willpower attack against all creatures within x spaces of the target space that does y damage.
Choose a space within 10 spaces. Make an intelligence vs willpower attack against all creatures within x spaces of the target space that does y damage. The damage from this attack ignores armor.
X allies within 6 spaces may make an attack.
Regain x health at the end of every round.
Gain advantage to defense agility rolls when adjacent to an ally that also has this feature.
Major movements do not draw opportunity attacks
Gain 1 damage dice to charge attacks and do not draw opportunity attacks when making major movements.
Gain 1 damage dice to charge attacks
Melee attacks have range 2 and can attack through allies.
Increase damage reduction by 2. All damage reduction is ignored when being attacked by blunt weapons.
If damaged by this creature, the target skips their next initiative card.
This creature gains toughness 10 against non-magical attacks.
Gain X mana and the ability to case mage spells.
Gain X mana and the ability to case diviner spells.
Choose a creature within 8 spaces and do 1d4 damage that ignores damage reduction
Choose a creature within 8 spaces and make an arcana vs. willpower attack that does 1d6 + arcana damage that ignores damage reduction.
Chose a space within 8 spaces. Make an arcana vs. willpower attack against all enemy creatures within 1 space that does 1d6 + arcana damage.
Enhance: Double the distance of the effect from the chosen space
Choose a space within 8 spaces to place a marker. Enemies that make any move into or out of any space within 2 spaces of the marker have all movements reduced by 1.
Choose a space within 8 spaces to place a marker. Allies within 2 spaces of the marker gain advantage to attacks and defense.
Heal a target creature within 8 spaces for 1d12 health.
Give all allies within 4 spaces advantage to agility and willpower defense rolls.
Choose a target enemy creature. All attacks made against that creature until the end of the next round gain bonus damage equal to this the diviners willpower.
The next ally within 4 spaces to be damaged by a spell ignores half the damage.

When creating an encounter, determine the monster profiles that you will use to represent each monster within the encounter and note down the level, type, health and features for those monsters. This encounter cheat sheet, plus the reference sheet for those monsters, will allow you to easily manage an encounter and track monster health and abilities.

Below you can see an example encounter card with some example monsters. At the end of the book, you will find a printable page of encounter cards that you can use to easily create and manage your own encounters.
Monsters of an even level (2, 4, 6, etc.) get bonus health equal to the following:

At the lowest levels, adventurers face weak but numerous foes such as goblins and kobolds, who rely on ambush tactics and traps to overwhelm their prey. Wolves and giant rats provide direct physical threats, lunging with snapping jaws or swarming in filthy packs, while skeletons offer a taste of the undead horrors to come, shambling forth with eerie resilience despite their brittle bones.
Small, cowardly goblins that hurl crude javelins from the shadows before fleeing.
Sneaky goblins that shoot small bows from the hidden places.
Goblins astride mangy wolves, charging with spears to harry adventurers.
A grizzled goblin barking orders, wielding a spear to direct ambushes.
Cackling goblins casting crude spells that erupt in chaotic bursts.
Hulking warriors with crude axes, delivering crushing blows in melee.
Shifty kobolds armed with knives and spears
Scrawny kobolds armed with slings, pelting foes from a safe distance.
Brittle undead that shamble forward, resilient to arrows despite their frailty.
Armed with rusty swords, these undead lash out tirelessly, tough against piercing blows.
Armed with rusty swords, these undead lash out tirelessly, tough against piercing blows.
Tiny, bat-like creatures that swarm from above, draining blood with needle-like proboscises.
Furious pack animal looking for vulnerable prey.
Filthy rodents that dart in for quick bites, overwhelming prey in swarms.
Multi-legged horrors that skitter up walls to deliver venomous bites.
Wall-crawling arachnids that leap to ensnare prey with venomous fangs.
Wall-crawling arachnid mother that sends her spawn to ensnare prey with venomous fangs.
Towering brutes swinging tree-trunk clubs, smashing anything in reach.

As adventurers grow stronger, they encounter more organized and dangerous foes like orcs and hobgoblins, who often employ crude tactics and wield better equipment than their weaker kin. Goblin wolf riders add mobility to the threat, charging into battle atop snarling beasts, while ghouls introduce paralyzing undead horrors, their clawing hands and hunger for flesh making them a sinister menace.
A battle-hardened orc wielding a heavy axe, ready to cleave through foes with extra attacks.
A frenzied orc that charges into battle, delivering devastating blows with reckless abandon.
A cunning orc leader who directs his warriors with brutal efficiency, allowing allies to make extra attacks
A disciplined hobgoblin armed with a halberd, capable of attacking from the second rank.
A skilled hobgoblin archer who can deliver precise, damaging shots from a distance.
A goblin mounted on a snarling wolf, using hit-and-run tactics to strike swiftly.
A large alpha wolf that leads its pack, allowing allied wolves to make extra attacks.
A ravenous undead that can scale walls and strike with paralyzing claws.
A well-trained human guard armed with a spear, capable of controlling the battlefield.
A graceful elf warrior who can deliver multiple precise strikes in combat.
A fierce gnoll that fights with furious anger.
A winged creature that can attack from above with screeching songs or talon strikes.
Towering brutes swinging tree-trunk clubs, smashing anything in reach.

At this stage, adventurers must contend with powerful brutes like ogres, whose sheer strength can crush the unwary with a single blow, and undead such as mummies and wights, which bring curses and life-draining attacks to sap the vitality of their foes. Harpies add an aerial threat, swooping down with enchanting songs to lure victims into danger before raking them with sharp talons.
A muscular, green-skinned humanoid with a fierce demeanor, wielding a large axe.
A towering, hulking creature with thick skin and a massive club, capable of crushing foes with ease.
A cunning and brutal ogre chieftain, barking orders to its underlings while wielding a spiked club.
A preserved corpse wrapped in linen, animated by necromantic magic to cast debilitating curses and summon swarms of insects.
A malevolent undead being that drains life with its touch and hurls bolts of dark energy.
An undead cavalryman on a skeletal horse, wielding a long lance to skewer foes from afar.
A lesser vampire with pale skin and elongated fangs, agile and deadly in close combat.
A winged monstrosity that uses its enchanting song to beguile and control its prey.
A regal harpy who inspires her kin with magical songs and heals their wounds with her touch.
A grotesque stone figure that can soar through the air and withstand arrows with its rocky hide.
A juvenile griffin, still growing into its full strength, but already a formidable predator with sharp beak and claws.

Adventurers at this level face formidable opponents such as heavily armored knights, skilled in combat and clad in steel, alongside packs of dire wolves, massive beasts with bone-crunching jaws. Hell hounds bring fiery breath to the battlefield, their scorching exhalations searing flesh, while owlbears combine the ferocity of bears and the predatory instincts of owls in a terrifying hybrid form.
A disciplined warrior clad in gleaming armor, wielding a lance and bow with precision.
A fiery canine that leaps through the air, unleashing bursts of flame on its foes.
A three-headed hound with thick hides, each head biting with enhanced force.
A shadowy entity that casts eerie spells and foresees enemy movements.
A gaunt figure cloaked in black, summoning undead minions to fight alongside them.
A necromancer encased in animated bones, unleashing waves of necrotic energy.
A swirling mass of wind and lightning, hurling bolts from above.
A reptilian horror with a petrifying gaze and a crushing tail swipe.
A hulking troll that clambers through trees and smashes with slimy fists.

At this level, adventurers encounter fearsome creatures like minotaur’s, whose brute strength and maze-dwelling habits make them deadly in close quarters. Lesser demons bring chaotic evil and supernatural abilities to the fight, their otherworldly presence unnerving, while medusas can petrify with a single gaze.
A hulking bull-headed creature that charges into battle with reckless abandon.
A frenzied minotaur warrior driven by bloodlust, striking with unmatched ferocity.
A small, winged imp that harasses foes with quick strikes from above.
A powerful demonic mage commanding dark magics and summoning hellish flames.
A tactical demon leader mounted on a nightmarish steed, directing its minions with ruthless efficiency.
A savage, bat-winged creature born from the abyss, tearing into enemies with razor-sharp claws.
A massive construct of rock and magic, delivering earth-shattering blows to any who dare approach.
An undead sorcerer of immense power, commanding legions of the dead and weaving necrotic spells.
A colossal being of immense strength, hurling boulders and stomping foes beneath its feet.

Adventurers must now face regenerating trolls, whose wounds heal rapidly unless burned or otherwise countered, and dragon wyrmlings, young but fierce with deadly breath weapons. Elementals bring the raw power of the planes—be it fire, earth, air, or water—shifting the battlefield with their primal might, while medusas turn careless foes to stone with their baleful stares.
A hulking troll adapted to the swamp, capable of climbing through the dense foliage and delivering powerful blows with its massive limbs.
A massive troll that roams the mountains, scaling cliffs with ease and smashing foes with earth-shattering blows
A fierce young red dragon that soars through the skies, blasts enemies with fire, and strikes with deadly precision.
A fierce red dragon wyrmling that has not yet learned to fly but is a furious foe to fight.
A towering giant that hurls boulders, smashes foes with its club, and charges into battle on the back of a massive beast, dealing extra damage.
A towering, hulking creature with thick skin and a massive club, capable of crushing foes with ease.
A devil that wields chains with deadly precision, striking from afar and delivering devastating blows that shred armor and flesh.

Adventurers face deadly flying predators like wyverns and chimera, the former wielding poisonous stings and the latter boasting multiple heads—lion, goat, and dragon—each attacking in unison. Sphinxes guard ancient secrets with their riddles and magical abilities, testing both mind and might, while drakes, lesser kin to dragons, bring their own fiery threats to the fray.
A young wyvern, still learning to hunt and fight. This weak and fragile hatchling relies on its wings to escape danger rather than engage in prolonged combat.
A wyvern with a venomous sting, capable of delivering deadly blows.
An ancient wyvern, its scales hardened by time, delivering crushing blows.
A monstrous creature with three heads, unleashing fire and fury upon its foes.
A winged sphinx that casts powerful spells to protect its domain and support its allies.
A snakefolk warrior that can scale walls and strike with its polearm from unexpected angles. Trained for combat, this agile fighter uses its spear to attack in formation or from elevated positions.
A cunning snakefolk leader that directs its troops and strikes with precision. This skilled commander boosts its allies’ attacks while delivering enhanced strikes of its own.
A swift centaur armed with a bow, charging into battle and firing arrows with deadly accuracy. Trained for mobility, this scout uses its equine speed to charge and unleash precise arrow volleys.
A powerful centaur leader that inspires its kin and leads charges into enemy lines. This commander enhances its herd’s assaults while leveraging its charge for devastating impact.
A centaur shaman that harnesses the power of nature and peers into the future to aid its allies. Fragile but mystical, this shaman casts spells and foresees events to support its tribe in battle.

Adventurers at this stage must contend with powerful entities such as greater demons, who wield dark magic and physical might with malevolent intent, and tree ents, ancient guardians of the forest whose tremendous strength can uproot entire groves. Werewolves bring the curse of lycanthropy and enhanced physical prowess, their howls chilling the night, while vine horrors ensnare and devour the unwary with writhing, thorny tendrils.
This towering predator uses its powerful jaws to crush prey and can scale rocky outcrops with surprising agility.
Clad in blackened armor, this fiendish warrior wields a wicked glaive to strike foes from a distance in formation.
A hulking undead knight from the infernal realms, its bony frame shrugs off arrows while delivering crushing blows.
A cackling fiend wreathed in flames; this sorcerer hurls explosive hellfire at clusters of enemies.
A disciplined elven warrior, this blade master wields a bow and longsword with deadly precision in quick succession.
A stern elven officer directs troops with tactical brilliance, striking from the second rank with a gleaming spear.
A fragile elven apprentice hurls bolts of arcane energy from a safe distance, supporting stronger allies.

At this high level, adventurers face massive foes like hill giants, whose sheer size and strength can devastate landscapes, and young dragons, whose breath weapons and flight make them lethal predators. Necromancers command armies of undead, weaving dark spells to bolster their forces, while death knights, fallen warriors cursed with undeath, wield both martial skill and unholy power.
A towering, brutish giant that charges down hills on a massive boar, smashing foes with relentless force.
A shrieking, bat-winged demon that hurls fiery blasts from above, scattering weak but numerous attacks across the battlefield.
A disciplined infernal warrior wielding a jagged glaive, striking from behind its allies with eerie resilience to arrows.
A colossal demon lord whose earth-shaking blows and fiery eruptions devastate entire groups of enemies.
A lithe, treelike predator that scales forest canopies, striking with precision guided by glimpses of the future.
A massive, gnarled tree-beast that uproots the ground with each swing, shrugging off spears with its woody hide.
A snarling orc rider atop a vicious boar, jabbing with a long spear and barking orders to its kin.
A cunning orc chieftain soaring on a tamed wyvern, weaving spells and directing brutal assaults from the sky.

Adventurers at this pinnacle face otherworldly beings such as angels, who wield divine power with radiant might, and vampires, masters of the night whose charm and regeneration make them relentless. Ancient mummies command curses and undead minions, their dusty tombs hiding untold dangers, while iron golems stand as nearly indestructible constructs, their metal fists smashing through any defense.
A colossal beast with razor-sharp fangs, capable of unleashing devastating attacks, area blasts, and hurling debris at foes.
A powerful undead lord with mastery over dark magic, able to command minions and soar through the air.
A mummified warrior wrapped in ancient bandages, skilled in combat with a polearm and resistant to piercing damage.
A swift and agile undead hound that can scale walls and strike with precision.
A regal mummified figure riding a spectral steed, directing its minions with strategic commands.
A sinister spellcaster who commands the sands, unleashing destructive storms and raising the dead.
A radiant celestial being that soars above the battlefield, inspiring allies and foreseeing enemy moves.

Adventurers encounter storm giants, whose command over storms and colossal strength can unleash devastating tempests across entire regions, and titans, ancient entities with godlike might that harkens back to a primordial era. Elder elementals embody the ultimate fury of fire, earth, air, or water, their presence alone capable of reshaping the world around them with elemental chaos. Colossal sea serpents rise from the ocean depths, their impenetrable scales and sheer size allowing them to crush ships and drag them into the abyss.
This colossal giant rides a tempestuous thundercloud, unleashing devastating lightning blasts and crushing enemies underfoot.
Clad in electrified armor and wielding a massive trident, this king directs the battlefield with foresight and authority.
A serpentine creature of bone and storm energy, striking with lethal precision and shrugging off piercing blows.
A ferocious winged predator crackling with electricity, diving from the skies to rend foes with charged claws.
A buzzing cloud of tiny, sparking motes that swarm and zap anything in their path with erratic bolts.
A humanoid mass of pure lightning, predicting enemy moves and striking with precision bolts from afar.

Heroes at this stage face vampire lords, whose mastery over darkness and hordes of undead servants make them nearly unkillable, regenerating wounds while enthralling even the strongest minds. Arch mages wield arcane power so vast they can twist reality itself, casting spells that defy the laws of nature, while liches, sustained by dark rituals, summon necromantic forces to overwhelm foes with armies of the dead. Pit fiends, supreme commanders of the infernal realms, blend overwhelming physical might with ruthless cunning, making them terrifying adversaries.
A frail skeletal warrior clad in tattered armor, hurling javelins from decayed hands.
A gaunt figure draped in rotting robes, weaving spells that shatter minds and bolster its allies with cryptic visions.
A colossal, rotting beast that unleashes a deafening roar to shatter foes, charging with unstoppable fury when ridden by its master.
A vicious fiend from the abyss, wielding a jagged glaive to carve through enemy lines with extra ferocity.
A horned general astride a flaming steed, barking orders to its minions while unleashing fiery bursts upon the battlefield.

At this legendary tier, adventurers confront adult dragons, whose devastating breath weapons can reduce armies to ash and whose armored scales shrug off all but the mightiest blows. Demon lords radiate chaotic evil, their corrupting influence capable of warping entire planes, while celestial champions, divine warriors of unmatched power, wield holy might that can smite evil with a single strike. These foes test the limits of even the greatest heroes with their awe-inspiring presence and abilities.
A massive dragon with ash-gray scales, it spews fiery breath and strikes with deadly precision from the skies.
A swift and ruthless killer cloaked in shimmering heat; it scales walls to deliver lethal, bone-shattering blows.
A cunning tactician wielding a blazing polearm, it directs allies with precision and unleashes fiery spells on foes.
A towering monstrosity of bone and shadow, it crushes entire groups with sweeping blows and shrugs off piercing strikes.

Adventurers reaching this level face krakens, whose massive tentacles shatter fleets and whose control over the seas conjures storms of apocalyptic scale, and ancient sea serpents, so vast they can coil around islands and swallow sea monsters whole. Colossal octopi crush coastal strongholds with their titanic limbs, while whirlpool elementals unleash torrents of water that drag entire navies into inescapable vortexes. These oceanic terrors dominate the seas with unmatched ferocity.
A colossal squid-like beast with tentacles that shatter ships and hurl deadly barrages of water and debris across the battlefield.
A sinuous, deep-sea predator that slithers up cliffs and strikes with lightning-fast bites, tearing through armor with ease.
A massive octopus that churns the seas into a maelstrom, crushing foes with its powerful arms and slamming multiple enemies at once.
A regal merfolk sovereign wielding ancient sea magic, calling forth waves to smite foes while inspiring allies to fight with renewed vigor.
A grotesque, undead vulture with tattered wings, diving from the skies to rip apart prey with enhanced, bone-shattering strikes.
A cunning, skeletal harpy-like creature that soars above the fray, directing its minions with eerie foresight and shrieking commands.

At the pinnacle of power, adventurers must battle ancient dragons, whose centuries of accumulated might make them living disasters, capable of annihilating kingdoms with a single roar, monstrous juggernauts whose regenerative hides and raw destructive force render them nearly invincible. Demon princes, sovereigns of the abyss, wield chaotic energies that can unravel reality itself, while archdevils, with their meticulous intellect and infernal legions, orchestrate schemes to dominate all existence. These ultimate foes represent the greatest challenges imaginable.
A colossal dragon with scales like shattered stone, its fiery breath and earth-shaking tail obliterate all in its path.
A towering construct of molten metal and bone, forged in infernal flames to crush foes with relentless force.
A cunning demonic general clad in spiked armor, directing its minions with dark magic and a wicked glaive.
A shadowy entity from the abyss, wielding eerie tendrils and prophetic curses to unravel its enemies’ fates.
Stay up to date with Heroic Adventures at
where you will find a character builder,
Thank you for playing Heroic Adventures!
